repos.name: grocery-helper
repos.purpose: German grocery-deal aggregator (FastAPI backend). A weekly cron POSTs /api/reset on the deployed backend, which re-scrapes Lidl Plus coupons and meinprospekt/Bonial weekly flyers via their JSON endpoints (httpx). Extraction is JSON-shape parsing (dict keys, a __NEXT_DATA__ regex), NOT DOM selectors.
repos.repo_notes: IMPORTANT: the failing cron exercises the DEPLOYED backend, not this machine — its datacenter IP may see different regional content than this Mac (a real past incident). If the local scrape yields real (non-sample) data while prod fails, do NOT guess-fix: report CANNOT_REPRODUCE with your analysis. The scrapers fall back to hardcoded sample data per source on failure; the failure signature is a store name suffix ' (sample)' and NULL offers.raw_payload.
repos.success_criteria: the local scrape writes >0 offers to backend/grocery.db with zero stores named '% (sample)' and zero offers with NULL raw_payload (the verify script asserts all three and prints the counts)

repos.name: clothing-sales-tracker
repos.purpose: pnpm monorepo tracking Uniqlo DE sale items with headless Playwright Chromium. A 6-hourly cron runs the scrapers in GitHub Actions and commits data/*.json snapshots to main (Actions-as-backend). Scrapers live in packages/scrapers/src/, one file per retailer.
repos.repo_notes: Failures raise a staged ScrapeError (stage: navigate | wait-for-tiles | scroll-paginate | extract) with expectedCount/actualCount and a ~4KB htmlSnippet, uploaded as a CI artifact — read it first. Brittle points: TILE_SELECTOR and COUNT_SELECTOR at the top of packages/scrapers/src/uniqlo.ts, and the in-page extraction fallback chain. The site hydrates on scroll, so tile counts below ~95% of the header count are treated as pagination failure.
repos.success_criteria: pnpm -r typecheck passes, pnpm scrape:uniqlo exits 0, and data/uniqlo-de-men.json contains products.length > 0 with a scrapedAt newer than the verify run's start (the verify script asserts all of this)
